blocks.body: Present Perfect: Questions and Short Answers — Have you...?, Has she...?, Yes, I have. / No, he hasn't.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In Present Perfect questions, we put have / has before the subject and use the main verb in V3.","rules":[{"label":"Have I / you / we / they ...?","color":"emerald","text":"With these subjects, start the question with have.","example":"Have you finished the task?"},{"label":"Has he / she / it ...?","color":"blue","text":"With he / she / it, use has.","example":"Has she called you?"},{"label":"Short answers","color":"amber","text":"In a short answer, repeat have / has or haven't / hasn't.","example":"Yes, I have. / No, he hasn't."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic question patterns","intro":"In a question, have or has comes first.","items":[{"label":"I / you / we / they","title":"Have ... + V3?","subtitle":"Have you seen this film? / Have they arrived?"},{"label":"he / she / it","title":"Has ... + V3?","subtitle":"Has he finished? / Has it started?"},{"label":"Yes","title":"Yes, ... have / has.","subtitle":"Yes, I have. / Yes, she has."},{"label":"No","title":"No, ... haven't / hasn't.","subtitle":"No, we haven't. / No, he hasn't."}]}

{"title":"2. Why we ask these questions","sections":[{"label":"About experience","color":"emerald","description":"Ask about experience up to now, often with ever.","examples":[{"en":"Have you ever been to Italy?","ua":"Experience up to now."},{"en":"Has she ever tried sushi?","ua":"Life experience without an exact time."}]},{"label":"About completion by now","color":"blue","description":"Ask if an action is already complete by the present moment.","examples":[{"en":"Have you finished your homework yet?","ua":"Completion up to now."},{"en":"Has he sent the email?","ua":"The result matters now."}]},{"label":"About recent news","color":"amber","description":"Ask about new information or a recent event that matters now.","examples":[{"en":"Have they arrived yet?","ua":"A recent update."},{"en":"Has the lesson started?","ua":"Something important now."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Question and short answer","intro":"In the answer, repeat have / has, not the whole sentence.","rows":[{"en":"Have you done the shopping?","ua":"Yes, I have. / No, I haven't.","note":"have → have / haven't"},{"en":"Has she called you?","ua":"Yes, she has. / No, she hasn't.","note":"has → has / hasn't"},{"en":"Have they ever been here before?","ua":"Yes, they have. / No, they haven't.","note":"experience up to now"}],"warning":"Do not say Has she calleded? or Yes, she have."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Putting has / have after the subject instead of before it.","wrong":"❌ She has finished?","right":"✅ Has she finished?"},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using the wrong auxiliary form.","wrong":"❌ Have he done it?","right":"✅ Has he done it?"},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Giving a short answer with the wrong form.","wrong":"❌ Yes, she have.","right":"✅ Yes, she has."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["A Present Perfect question = Have / Has + subject + V3.","Use have with I / you / we / they.","Use has with he / she / it.","In short answers, repeat have / has or haven't / hasn't.","After have / has, the main verb stays in V3."]}
